name: Low-Fat Caramel Custard
recipeIngredient: 1/2 package egg beaters or 99% egg substitute (4 eggs)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Place water on heat.
3. Prepare a bain marie (water bath) by placing 8 pyrex custard cups or coffee mugs in a 3-inch deep roasting pan. Cover the bottom of the pan with cold water.
4. Scald the lemon zest and skim milk in a saucepan.
5. Caramelize 3/8 cup sugar in a saucepan on high heat until it melts and turns brown. Stir constantly to avoid burns.
6. Pour about 2 tablespoons caramelized sugar into each custard cup, allowing it to solidify. Beat the eggs, remaining sugar, and salt together. Strain the scalded milk to remove lemon zest.
7. Mix the egg mixture with the strained milk. Add vanilla extract. Fill cups up to 1/4 inch from the top (about 3/4 to 1 inch deep).
8. Place the pan in the preheated oven and add water until it reaches 1/4 inch above the custards. Bake for 45 minutes or until the custard firms up and browns slightly on top, without overcooking.
9. Let cool to room temperature before refrigerating.
10. To serve, cut around the top of each custard cup and invert onto a small plate. Spoon caramel sauce into the bottom of the cup and garnish with fresh mint leaves.
